examples.title: Polymorphism with Virtual Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates how base class pointer to derived object calls derived function when virtual
examples.explanation: Base pointer holds Derived object. Virtual show() causes Derived::show() to be called at runtime. Virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup order.

examples.title: Abstract Class with Pure Virtual Function
examples.description: Shows how abstract classes define interfaces using pure virtual functions
examples.explanation: Shape is abstract with pure virtual area(). Rectangle and Circle provide implementations. Base pointer array demonstrates polymorphism - same area() call produces different results.

examples.title: Dynamic Cast for Safe Downcasting
examples.description: Demonstrates runtime type checking with dynamic_cast
examples.explanation: dynamic_cast performs runtime type checking. First cast succeeds because bptr actually points to Derived object. Second cast fails because bptr2 points to Base object, returning nullptr.

examples.title: Object Slicing Demonstration
examples.description: Shows what happens when derived object is assigned to base object by value
examples.explanation: When Derived object d is assigned to Base object b by value, only the Base part is copied. The b member is lost - this is object slicing. Using pointers or references prevents slicing.
